questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Condensation of water vapour into water is influenced by which of the following factor/factors?
1Volume of air
2.Humidity
3.Temperature
Select the answer using the code given below:
questions.solution:
Condensation occurs when the partial pressure of water vapour reaches its saturation value. That process can be driven by

changing the air volume (hence its pressure),

the existing humidity (vapour content), and

the temperature (which sets the saturation vapour pressure).

Hence all three factors are involved.

Answer: Option 4 (1, 2 and 3)

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following equations related to the motion of an object is NOT correct? (Symbols carry their usual meanings)
options: u = v − at
u2 − v2 = 2as
Distance traveled during the nth second = u + (1/2)a(2n - 1)
questions.solution:
The equation that’s NOT correct is Option 3. The standard “third” equation of motion is

$$v^2 = u^2 + 2as$$

which can be rearranged as

$$u^2 - v^2 = -\,2as$$

—not $$u^2 - v^2 = 2as\,. $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A bullet of mass 10 g is horizontally fired with velocity 300 m s<sup>-1</sup> from a pistol of mass 1 kg. What is the recoil velocity of the pistol?
questions.solution:
To determine the recoil velocity of the pistol, we can use the principle of conservation of momentum. According to this principle, the total momentum before and after firing the bullet must be the same because there are no external forces acting on the system.

Given:

  • Mass of the bullet, $ m_b = 10 \, \text{g} = 0.01 \, \text{kg} $
  • Velocity of the bullet, $ v_b = 300 \, \text{m/s} $
  • Mass of the pistol, $ m_p = 1 \, \text{kg} $
  • Recoil velocity of the pistol, $ v_p = ? $

The total momentum before firing is zero because both the bullet and the pistol are at rest initially. Therefore, the total momentum after firing should also be zero.

Let us denote the recoil velocity of the pistol as $ v_p $. The momentum of the bullet and the pistol after firing are given by:

$$ \begin{align*} \text{Momentum of the bullet} & = m_b \cdot v_b \\ \text{Momentum of the pistol} & = m_p \cdot v_p \end{align*} $$

According to the conservation of momentum:

$$ m_b \cdot v_b + m_p \cdot v_p = 0 $$

Solving for $ v_p $:

$$ v_p = -\frac{m_b \cdot v_b}{m_p} $$

Substituting the given values:

$$ \begin{align*} v_p &= -\frac{0.01 \, \text{kg} \times 300 \, \text{m/s}}{1 \, \text{kg}} \\ v_p &= -3 \, \text{m/s} \end{align*} $$

Thus, the recoil velocity of the pistol is $ -3 \, \text{m/s} $. The negative sign indicates that the pistol moves in the opposite direction to that of the bullet.

Hence, the correct option is:

Option C: -3 m s-1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A person can see nearby objects clearly but cannot see distant object distinctly, the person is suffering with
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\text{Option 3: Myopia}$$

Step by step:

  • In myopia (short-sightedness), a person can see nearby objects clearly.

  • But distant objects appear blurred.

So the given condition matches myopia.

Final answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Myopia}}$$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following regarding density of water at atmospheric pressure is correct?
options: Density of water at 4°C is 1000 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 0°C is 1000 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 0°C is 100 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
Density of water at 4°C is 10 kg/m<sup>3</sup>.
questions.solution:
Option A is correct.

The density of water at 4°C is approximately 1000 kg/m³. At this temperature, water is at its maximum density. This value is derived from the definition of the kilogram, which was historically based on the mass of 1 liter of water at its maximum density point, or 4°C.

Water exhibits anomalous expansion, meaning that it reaches its maximum density not at the freezing point (0°C) but slightly above it at 4°C. Below 4°C, water begins to expand as it approaches the freezing point. This is one reason why ice floats on water—because its density is less than that of liquid water.

The density of water decreases as the temperature increases beyond 4°C due to thermal expansion. Therefore, the understanding of water's density at various temperatures is crucial for many scientific and engineering applications.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A block of wood 40 cm × 20 cm × 10 cm) is kept on a tabletop in three different positions: (a) with its side of dimensions 20 cm × 10 cm; (b) with its side of dimensions 10 cm × 40 cm; and (c) with its side of dimensions 40 cm × 20 cm. The pressure exerted by the wooden block on the tabletop in these positions is represented by P<sub>A</sub>, P<sub>B</sub> and P<sub>c</sub>, respectively. The pressure follows the trend
options: PA > PB > Pc
PA < PB < Pc
PA = PB = Pc
PA < PB = Pc
questions.solution:
To determine the pressure exerted by the wooden block on the tabletop in each of the three positions, we need to use the formula for pressure:

$$ P = \frac{F}{A} $$

where $ P $ is the pressure, $ F $ is the force (in this case, the weight of the block), and $ A $ is the area over which the force is applied. This implies that the pressure exerted by the block on the tabletop is inversely proportional to the area in contact with the surface.

The weight $ F $ of the block remains constant in all positions. Therefore, we only need to change the area of contact $ A $ for each position to determine the pressure.

Let's evaluate the area for each position:

Position A:
Dimensions in contact: 20 cm × 10 cm

Area$ A_A = 20 \times 10 = 200 \, \text{cm}^2 $

Position B:
Dimensions in contact: 10 cm × 40 cm

Area$ A_B = 10 \times 40 = 400 \, \text{cm}^2 $

Position C:
Dimensions in contact: 40 cm × 20 cm

Area$ A_C = 40 \times 20 = 800 \, \text{cm}^2 $

Now, calculate the pressure for each position:

$$ P_A = \frac{F}{A_A} = \frac{F}{200 \, \text{cm}^2} $$

$$ P_B = \frac{F}{A_B} = \frac{F}{400 \, \text{cm}^2} $$

$$ P_C = \frac{F}{A_C} = \frac{F}{800 \, \text{cm}^2} $$

Since the pressure $ P $ is inversely proportional to the area $ A $, the smallest area will exert the greatest pressure, and the largest area will exert the least pressure. Therefore, the pressures will follow this trend:

$$ P_A > P_B > P_C $$

This corresponds to Option A:

PA > PB > Pc

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: If three resistors of 1 Ohm each connect in parallel to each other the resultant resistance is
questions.solution:
For resistors in parallel, the reciprocal of the total resistance is the sum of reciprocals:

$$ \frac1{R_{\rm eq}} = \frac1{R_1} + \frac1{R_2} + \frac1{R_3} = \frac1{1} + \frac1{1} + \frac1{1} = 3 $$

So

$$ R_{\rm eq} = \frac1{3}\,\Omega. $$

That corresponds to Option 2: 1/3 Ohm.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the best shape of a solid metal rod to form the top end of a lightning conductor?
questions.solution: Lightning rods are always pointed to attract charges towards it.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following about different frictional forces is correct?
questions.solution:
Let's examine and compare the different types of friction: static friction, kinetic friction, and rolling friction.

Static Friction : This is the friction that exists between a stationary object and the surface it's on. It needs to be overcome to start moving the object. It is generally the highest among the three because it involves overcoming the intermolecular forces between the surfaces.

Kinetic Friction : This is the friction that acts between moving surfaces. It is typically lower than static friction because once an object is in motion, less force is required to keep it moving compared to starting the motion.

Rolling Friction : This is the friction that occurs when an object rolls over a surface. Rolling friction is generally much smaller than both static and kinetic friction because the area of contact between the two surfaces is much smaller.

Therefore, the correct order from highest to lowest is generally :

$$\text{Static friction} > \text{Kinetic friction} > \text{Rolling friction}$$

Based on this understanding, the correct choice is :

Option C : Static friction > Kinetic friction > Rolling friction

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
Consider the following statements about Light year:

1. Light year is a unit for measurement of very large distances.

2. Light year is a unit for measurement of very large time intervals.

3. Light year is a unit for measurement of intensity of light.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Only statement 1 is correct.

A light year is a unit of distance, not time or intensity of light. It is defined as the distance that light travels in a vacuum in one year. The speed of light is approximately $299,792,458$ meters per second, so over the course of a year, this distance is about:

$$ 1 \text{ light year} = 9.461 \times 10^{15} \text{ meters} $$

Thus, the correct option is D: 1 only.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is true for sound waves propa- gating in air?
options: Sound is an electromagnetic wave and transverse in nature
Sound is a mechanical wave and longitudinal in nature
Sound is a mechanical wave and transverse in nature
Sound is an electromagnetic wave and longitudinal in nature
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Sound is a mechanical wave and longitudinal in nature.

Here's why:

Mechanical Waves:

  • Mechanical waves require a medium to travel. They are disturbances that travel through a medium by transferring energy from one particle to the next.
  • Sound waves are mechanical because they need air, water, solids, or any other medium to propagate.

Longitudinal Waves:

  • In longitudinal waves, the particles of the medium vibrate parallel to the direction of wave propagation. This means that the particles move back and forth in the same direction that the wave is traveling.
  • Sound waves are longitudinal because the air molecules vibrate back and forth in the same direction that the sound wave is traveling.

Why other options are incorrect:

  • Option A and D: Electromagnetic waves do not require a medium to travel. They can travel through a vacuum, like space. Examples of electromagnetic waves include light, radio waves, and X-rays.
  • Option C: Transverse waves are waves where the particles of the medium vibrate perpendicular to the direction of wave propagation. An example of a transverse wave is a wave on a string.

In summary, sound waves are mechanical waves that travel through a medium and are longitudinal because the particles of the medium vibrate parallel to the direction of wave propagation.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is primarily responsible for conduction of current in a metal?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option B

Free electrons

To understand this, let's delve into the nature of electrical conduction in metals. Metals are characterized by their unique atomic structure, which includes a lattice of positively charged ions surrounded by a 'sea' of free electrons. These free electrons are not bound to any particular atom and are free to move throughout the metal. This freedom of movement is a critical property that allows metals to conduct electric current efficiently.

When an electric field is applied to a metal, these free electrons are influenced by the field and start drifting in the direction opposite to the field. This movement of free electrons constitutes the electric current. The bound electrons, on the other hand, are tightly held by their respective atoms and cannot move freely to conduct current. Ions in a metal lattice are also stationary and do not contribute to the conduction of current. Therefore, it is primarily the free electrons that are responsible for the conduction of current in a metal.

For a quantitative understanding, we can refer to Ohm's Law, which states:

$$V = IR$$

Where:

$$V$$ is the voltage across the conductor,

$$I$$ is the current through the conductor,

$$R$$ is the resistance of the conductor.

In the context of metals, the low resistance is largely due to the high density of free electrons that can move with minimal opposition, thus allowing a significant current to flow with a small applied voltage.

Therefore, free electrons play the crucial role in conducting current in metallic materials.

questions.questionText: The magnetic field produced by a current-carrying straight wire at a point outside the wire depends
options: inversely on the distance from it

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without changing the temperature, is called
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option 2: specific latent heat.

Explanation:

The quantity of heat needed to change unit mass of a substance from liquid to vapour without any change in temperature is called specific latent heat of vaporisation.

In NCERT terms:

  • Latent heat: Heat absorbed or released during change of state.

  • Specific latent heat: Heat required for unit mass of a substance to change its state without change in temperature.

So here, since the change is from liquid to vapour, it is specific latent heat.

Therefore,

$$ \text{Correct option} = \text{Option 2} $$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: What is the magnification produced by a concave lens of focal length 10 cm, when an image is formed at a distance of 5 cm from the lens?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText:
When a solid body is partially or completely immersed in a fluid, the fluid exerts an upward force on the body. The magnitude of the force is equal to
1.the mass of the body
2.the weight of the displaced fluid by the body
Which of the above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
The correct choice is 2 only.

By Archimedes’ principle, the buoyant force equals the weight of the fluid displaced:

$$F_{\rm b} = \rho_{\rm fluid}\,V_{\rm disp}\,g$$

where

• ρ₍fluid₎ is the fluid’s density

• V₍disp₎ is the volume of fluid displaced

• g is the acceleration due to gravity.

It is not equal to the mass of the body.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following scientists mentioned that an electron in an atom could revolve in certain stable orbits without the emission of radiant energy?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Niels Bohr.

Niels Bohr was a pioneering physicist who made fundamental contributions to our understanding of atomic structure and quantum mechanics. In 1913, Bohr introduced his model of the atom, which incorporated some aspects of classical physics and early quantum theories. One of the key postulates of the Bohr model is that electrons can revolve around the nucleus of an atom in specific, quantized orbits without the emission of radiant energy. These stable orbits correspond to certain fixed energy levels.

Bohr proposed that while the electron remains in one of these discrete energy levels, no energy is radiated. Radiant energy is emitted or absorbed only when an electron transitions from one energy level to another. This idea was revolutionary because, according to classical electromagnetic theory, an accelerating charge such as an electron in orbit should continuously emit radiation and lose energy, leading to a collapse of the orbit. Bohr's model provided a way to circumvent this problem by introducing quantum concepts.

Bohr's theory can be summarized by the equation for the quantized angular momentum of an electron:

$$L = n \hbar$$

where:

  • $$L$$ is the angular momentum
  • $$n$$ is a positive integer (quantum number)
  • $$\hbar$$ is the reduced Planck's constant ( $$\hbar = \frac{h}{2 \pi}$$ )

This discovery was critical in the development of quantum mechanics and helped to explain phenomena that could not be accounted for by classical physics alone. It laid the groundwork for future atomic models and a more comprehensive understanding of atomic and subatomic processes.

In conclusion, Niels Bohr's concept of electrons revolving in quantized stable orbits without radiating energy was fundamental in explaining atomic stability and the discrete nature of atomic spectra.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following radiations has longer wavelength ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 3: Micro Wave

Reason:

In the electromagnetic spectrum, wavelength increases in this order:

$$ \text{X-rays} < \text{Ultraviolet} < \text{Infra-red} < \text{Microwaves} $$

So, among the given options, microwaves have the longest wavelength.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: The length of a simple pendulum is increased four times to its previous value while the mass is doubled. What is the ratio of the new and previous time period of the pendulum?
questions.solution:
Calculation:

The time period of a simple pendulum is given by the formula:

$ T_{\text{old}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

If the length $ L $ of the pendulum is increased four times and the mass is doubled, we note that the time period is independent of the pendulum's mass. Therefore, the time period depends only on the length and gravitational acceleration.

For the new pendulum:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{4L}{g}} $

Simplifying this expression:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2\pi\sqrt{4}\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2 \times 2\pi\sqrt{\frac{L}{g}} $

Thus, comparing the initial and new time periods:

$ T_{\text{new}} = 2T_{\text{old}} $

The ratio of the new to the old time period is:

$ T_{\text{new}} : T_{\text{old}} = 2 : 1 $

Therefore, the ratio of the new and previous time periods is 2:1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: A boy of mass 52 kg jumps with a horizontal velocity of 2 m/s onto a stationary cart of mass 3 kg. The cart is fixed with frictionless wheels. Which one of the following would be the speed of the cart?

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about surface tension is correct ?
options: It will remain same with increase or decrease of temperature
It will always decrease with increase or decrease of temperature
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 2: It decreases when temperature increases.

Explanation:

Surface tension is the property of a liquid surface due to which it behaves like a stretched membrane.

When temperature increases, the molecules of the liquid gain more kinetic energy and move more freely. Because of this, the intermolecular force of attraction becomes less effective at the surface, so the surface tension decreases.

Therefore:

$$ \text{Surface tension decreases with increase in temperature} $$

So, the correct option is Option 2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.questionText: Which one of the following wavelength radiations may correspond to an X-ray ?
questions.solution:
X-rays have very short wavelengths, usually in the range of about

$$ 0.01 \text{ nm to } 10 \text{ nm} $$

Now check the options:

  • $1 \text{ nm}$: lies in the X-ray range

  • $10 \text{ nm}$: at the extreme end, but usually not taken as the standard answer here

  • $100 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

  • $300 \text{ nm}$: ultraviolet region

So, the correct answer is:

$$ \boxed{1 \text{ nm}} $$

Option 1.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Toothpaste prevents tooth decay by:
options: making a coat of calcium over the teeth surface.
questions.solution:
Toothpaste helps prevent tooth decay primarily through:

Option C: the action of fluoride.

Fluoride in toothpaste helps to strengthen tooth enamel and make it more resistant to the acids produced by bacteria in the mouth, which can cause decay. Additionally, some toothpastes may help to neutralize acids (Option A), but the primary mechanism is through the remineralization effect of fluoride.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following materials is not an allotrope of carbon ?
questions.solution:
Option C: Fly ash is not an allotrope of carbon.

Allotropes of carbon are different structural forms of the same element, where the atoms are bonded together in different patterns or arrangements. Here, we can explore the given choices:

Diamond: In diamond, each carbon atom tetrahedrally coordinates with four other carbon atoms in a 3-dimensional network, making it an allotrope with a rigid structure.

Graphene: Graphene consists of a single layer of carbon atoms arranged in a two-dimensional honeycomb lattice, making it one of the known allotropes of carbon.

Fly ash: This is a byproduct from burning pulverized coal in electric power generating plants. It primarily consists of silicon dioxide (SiO₂), aluminum oxide (Al₂O₃), and iron oxide (Fe₂O₃) but doesn't have any specific arrangement of carbon atoms, hence is not considered an allotrope of carbon.

Fullerene: Fullerenes are molecules of carbon atoms arranged in a closed shell, typically in spherical, ellipsoidal, or cylindrical shapes, and are indeed allotropes of carbon.

In summary, amongst the options provided, fly ash does not qualify as an allotrope of carbon.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText:
In the following redox reaction, which compound is acting as a reducing agent ?

N2H4 + 2H2O2 → N2 + 4H2O
questions.solution:
To find the reducing agent, we check which substance gets oxidised.

Given reaction:

$$ N_2H_4 + 2H_2O_2 \rightarrow N_2 + 4H_2O $$

Step 1: Find oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2H_4$

Let oxidation state of nitrogen be $x$.

In $N_2H_4$:

  • Hydrogen has oxidation state $+1$

So,

$$ 2x + 4(+1) = 0 $$

$$ 2x + 4 = 0 $$

$$ 2x = -4 $$

$$ x = -2 $$

So, oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2H_4$ is $-2$.

Step 2: Find oxidation state of nitrogen in $N_2$

In elemental form,

$$ \text{Oxidation state of } N \text{ in } N_2 = 0 $$

So nitrogen changes from:

$$ -2 \rightarrow 0 $$

This is oxidation.

Step 3: Identify reducing agent

The substance which gets oxidised acts as the reducing agent.

Here, $N_2H_4$ is oxidised to $N_2$.

Therefore, the reducing agent is:

$$ \boxed{N_2H_4} $$

Correct option: 2. $N_2H_4$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Refining of petroleum is carried out using which one of the following techniques?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Hydrogenation of vegetable oils using nickel catalyst is an example of
questions.solution:
The hydrogenation of vegetable oils using a nickel catalyst is an example of an addition reaction.

In the hydrogenation process, hydrogen molecules ($H_2$) are added to the unsaturated carbon-carbon double bonds ($C=C$) in the fatty acid chains of vegetable oils. This addition of hydrogen atoms converts double bonds into single bonds, effectively saturating the oil and turning it into a solid or semi-solid form, like margarine or shortening.

The generalized equation for this reaction is:

$$ \text{RCH=CHR} + \text{H}_2 \xrightarrow{\text{Ni}} \text{RCH}_2\text{CH}_2\text{R} $$

Here, $ \text{RCH=CHR} $ represents an unsaturated fatty acid with a double bond, and $\text{Ni}$ is the nickel catalyst that facilitates the reaction by lowering the activation energy, enabling the hydrogen molecules to add across the double bond more efficiently.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is NOT a heterogeneous mixture ?
options: Mixture of sugar 8 and salt crystals
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 1: Sulphur in carbon disulphide}}$$

Step-by-step:

A heterogeneous mixture has non-uniform composition, and its different parts can be seen separately.

Now check each option:

  1. Sulphur in carbon disulphide

    Sulphur dissolves completely in carbon disulphide, so it forms a homogeneous mixture, not a heterogeneous mixture.

  2. Mixture of sugar and salt crystals

    Sugar crystals and salt crystals remain separate, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

  3. Sandy water

    Sand does not dissolve in water, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

  4. Sulphur in water

    Sulphur does not dissolve in water, so it is a heterogeneous mixture.

Therefore, the one which is not a heterogeneous mixture is:

$$\boxed{\text{Sulphur in carbon disulphide}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is not a pigment?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements is NOT correct ?
options: Diamond is hard due to extended covalent bonding in it
Fullerene is composed of five and six membered rings
Diamond is a good conductor of electricity
Graphite is used as a lubricant
questions.solution:
The incorrect statement is:

Option 3: Diamond is a good conductor of electricity

Step-by-step explanation:

1. Diamond

In diamond, each carbon atom is bonded to four other carbon atoms by strong covalent bonds, forming a rigid three-dimensional network.

So:

  • Diamond is very hard

  • This is due to extended covalent bonding

  • But diamond does not conduct electricity, because it has no free electrons

So Option 1 is correct.

2. Fullerene

Fullerene is a form of carbon made up of five-membered and six-membered rings.

So Option 2 is correct.

3. Graphite

Graphite has layers of carbon atoms. These layers can slide over one another easily.

So:

  • Graphite is used as a lubricant

  • It also conducts electricity due to the presence of free electrons

So Option 4 is correct.

Final Answer:

$$\boxed{\text{Option 3}}$$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: For acid rain, the pH of rain water should be less than
questions.solution:
Acid rain is defined as precipitation with a pH lower than the natural “background” value of about 5.6 (due to dissolved CO₂).

So the correct choice is:

Option 3: 5·6

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following reactions is an example of decomposition reaction?
options: CH4 (g) + 2O2 (g) → CO2(g) + 2H2O(g)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Water gas is a mixture of:
questions.solution:
Water gas is a mixture of carbon monoxide (CO) and hydrogen (H₂). It is produced by passing steam over hot carbon (coke) at high temperatures, typically around 1000°C. The chemical reaction for the formation of water gas is as follows:

$$\text{C} + \text{H}_2\text{O} \rightarrow \text{CO} + \text{H}_2$$

Given this information, the correct option is:

Option B: CO and H₂

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: For manufacturing of glass, which among the following is used as a source of silica?
questions.solution:
For the manufacturing of glass, the source of silica used is:

Option D: Sand

Sand is primarily composed of silicon dioxide (SiO₂), which is the main ingredient in glass production. The silicon dioxide from sand melts at high temperatures and then cools to form glass.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: An iron nail dipped in copper sulphate solution turns brown. This is due to which one of the following types of reactions?
questions.solution:
The iron nail turning brown when dipped in copper sulphate solution is due to the following reaction:

$$ \text{Fe (s)} + \text{CuSO}_4 \text{(aq)} \rightarrow \text{FeSO}_4 \text{(aq)} + \text{Cu (s)} $$

In this reaction, iron displaces copper from copper sulphate solution, forming iron sulphate and depositing copper metal on the nail, which is observed as a brown layer.

Thus, the correct type of reaction is:

Option D: Displacement reaction

questions.questionText: Which one of the following metals floats in cold water?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The mass number of argon is 40. Which one of the following statements is correct?
options: The number of protons in argon is 22.
The number of neutrons in argon is 18.
The number of electrons in argon is 18.
The sum of numbers of protons and electrons in argon is 40.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following salts does not possess water of crystallization?
questions.solution:
Water of crystallization refers to the water molecules that are part of the crystalline structure of a salt. Many salts incorporate these water molecules into their solid structure, indicating that they possess water of crystallization. Let's look at each option:

  • Option A: Potassium permanganate

    • Potassium permanganate ($KMnO_4$) is a salt that does not incorporate water molecules into its crystal structure. It is generally found in its anhydrous form.

  • Option B: Blue vitriol

    • Blue vitriol is the common name for copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate ($CuSO_4 \cdot 5H_2O$). It clearly possesses water of crystallization, as indicated by the "pentahydrate" suffix, which means it contains five molecules of water of crystallization.

  • Option C: Washing soda

    • Washing soda is sodium carbonate decahydrate ($Na_2CO_3 \cdot 10H_2O$). The "decahydrate" suffix indicates that it has ten water molecules of crystallization.

  • Option D: Mohr’s salt

    • Mohr's salt is ammonium iron(II) sulfate hexahydrate ($(NH_4)_2Fe(SO_4)_2 \cdot 6H_2O$). The "hexahydrate" suffix indicates it contains six molecules of water of crystallization.

Based on the descriptions above, the correct option is:

Option A: Potassium permanganate, as it does not possess water of crystallization.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following type of medicines is used for treating indigestion ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is:

Option 4: Antacid

Explanation:

Indigestion is usually caused by excess acid in the stomach. Antacids are medicines that help to neutralise the extra acid and give relief from indigestion.

Other options:

  • Analgesic: used to relieve pain

  • Antiseptic: used to prevent infection in wounds

  • Antibiotic: used to kill or stop the growth of bacteria

So, the medicine used for treating indigestion is Antacid.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following are essential constituents of Portland cement?
questions.solution:
Portland cement is a key material in construction and its essential ingredients must contribute to its properties such as strength, setting time, and durability. Let's break down the components of each option to determine the correct answer:

Option A: Sand, lime, clay

While sand, lime, and clay are important materials in construction, not all of these are essential constituents of Portland cement. Sand is an aggregate used in concrete, but it is not a key ingredient in the cement itself.

Option B: Silica, alumina, lime

This option lists silica, alumina, and lime. These are indeed the principal constituents of Portland cement:

  • Silica ($$SiO_2$$): Provides strength to cement.
  • Alumina ($$Al_2O_3$$): Imparts quick setting ability to the cement.
  • Lime ($$CaO$$): Primary ingredient that reacts with water to form calcium silicate hydrates, giving cement its strength.

Option C: Silica, lime, graphite powder

Graphite powder is not a constituent of Portland cement, which makes this option incorrect.

Option D: Sand, graphene, clay

This option includes sand and graphene, neither of which are required in the chemical composition of Portland cement.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: Silica, alumina, lime

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following allotropes of carbon is isomorphous with crystalline silicon?

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Element X forms a chloride with the formula XCl₂, which is a solid with high melting point. X would most likely be in the same group of periodic table as
questions.solution:
XCl₂ being a high-melting solid tells us it’s an ionic compound with X in the +2 oxidation state. Metals that commonly form MCl₂ salts are the Group 2 (alkaline-earth) elements.

• Group 2 elements → Be, Mg, Ca, …

• From the options given, Mg is the only Group 2 metal.

Answer: Option 3 (Mg)

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: The elemental composition of diamond is
questions.solution: Diamond is actually a fascinating material because it's an allotrope of carbon. This means it's made up entirely of carbon atoms, but they're arranged in a very specific, strong crystal structure. This unique arrangement of carbon atoms is what gives diamond its incredible hardness and brilliance!

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Who among the following is not a recipient of Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 2022?
questions.solution:
1. Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2022 was awarded jointly to

    a. Carolyn R. Bertozzi

    b. Morten Meldal

    c. K. Barry Sharpless

  2. Benjamin List received the Nobel Prize in Chemistry in 2021 (for organocatalysis), not in 2022.

  3. Therefore, the one who is not a 2022 recipient is Option B: Benjamin List.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which among the following is the correct composition of Borax?
questions.solution:
Borax, also known as sodium borate, is a mineral and a salt of boric acid. Its chemical composition includes sodium (Na), boron (B), oxygen (O), and hydrogen (H). The correct formula for Borax is $$\text{Na}_2\text{B}_4\text{O}_7\cdot10\text{H}_2\text{O}$$.

Given the options, the correct composition of Borax is:

Option B

Sodium, Boron, Oxygen and Hydrogen

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following is the chemical formula of Hypobromous acid?
questions.solution:
The chemical formula for Hypobromous acid is:

Option B: $$ \text{HOBr} $$

Hypobromous acid is a weak, unstable acid and is a bromine analog of hypochlorous acid. It can be represented as $$ \text{HOBr} $$, where the hydrogen atom is covalently bonded to an oxygen atom, which is in turn bonded to a bromine atom. This compound is typically formed by the reaction of bromine with water.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.questionText: Which one of the following statements about a compound is NOT correct?
options: A compound is a substance composed of two or more elements, chemically combined in a fixed proportion
Properties of a compound are different from its constituent elements
A compound is an impure substance
The constituents of a compound can be separated only by chemical or electrochemical reactions
questions.solution:
The statement that is NOT correct is Option 3:

“A compound is an impure substance.”

• By definition, a compound is a pure substance made up of two or more elements chemically combined in fixed proportions.

• Its properties differ from those of the individual elements.

• You can only separate its constituents by chemical or electrochemical means.
